ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Pangoro'; depicted figure (large dark-grey panda creature with white face markings, leaf in mouth, powerful build, rocky setting) matches the canonical Fighting/Dark Gen 6 Pokemon, National Pokedex no. 675. Verified via card name, PokemonTCG.io metadata, Bulbapedia: Pangoro, and flavor text cross-reference.

trivia_bullets.text: Pangoro is National Pokedex no. 675, a Fighting/Dark-type introduced in Generation VI (X/Y, 2013). It is Pancham's evolution and gains the Dark type at evolution — the game explicitly frames this as a 'bad influence' from the Dark-type party member required to trigger the evolution. [Bulbapedia: Pangoro; Pokemondb.net; via WebSearch]

The bamboo shoot Pangoro perpetually holds in its mouth is functional: it detects minute vibrations and movements from opponents through the leaf's sensitivity, giving Pangoro a sensory edge in combat. The design draws from bancho — Japanese juvenile-delinquent gang leaders — combined with giant panda aesthetics and traditional Chinese/Japanese bamboo symbolism. [Bulbapedia: Pangoro design section; WebSearch snippet]

Flavor text on this card: "This rowdy Pokemon boasts great physical strength. Many Trainers are also smitten by its lively character." A tonal contrast to its intimidating bulk — Pangoro is described as having a 'lively character' that attracts fans despite its aggressive reputation. [PokemonTCG.io: sm12-120 flavorText; frontmatter capture]

Pangoro on this card has two attacks: Light Punch (40 for 1 Colorless) and Magnum Punch (90 for 3 Colorless). The straightforward Fighting-archetype moveset is consistent with its in-game role as a physical attacker — base Attack of 124 made Pangoro a notable physical threat in Gen 6 competitive play. [PokemonTCG.io: sm12-120 oracle_text; frontmatter capture; WebSearch re: Pangoro competitive Gen 6]
